LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT EFOLDER, INC. DEMURRER TO ENTIRE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITH TWENTY DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. MR. MILLER IS GIVEN LEAVE TO AMEND HIS COMPLAINT TO ALLEGE ACTIONS THAT OCCURRED WITHIN THE SCOPE OF MR. RICHMAN'S EMPLOYMENT AND THAT MR. RICHMAN WAS UNFIT TO WORK AS A SALESPERSON, IF HE CAN DO SO IN GOOD FAITH. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: A. JAMES ROBERTSON II; CLERK: FELICIA GREEN; COURT REPORTER: GENA EALES, CSR NO. 9176, EMAIL: GEALES@SFTC.ORG; DEPT. 302.

MOTION TO STRIKE PRAYER FOR PUNITIVE DAMAGES FROM FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS OFF CALENDAR IN LIGHT OF THE COURT'S RULING ON EFOLDER, INC.'S DEMURRER, WITHOUT PREJUDICE TO BEING REFILED AFTER MR. MILLER AMENDS HIS COMPLAINT. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: A. JAMES ROBERTSON II; CLERK: FELICIA GREEN; COURT REPORTER: GENA EALES, CSR NO. 9176, EMAIL: GEALES@SFTC.ORG; DEPT. 302.

LAW AND MOTION, 302, DEFENDANT EFOLDER, INC.'S MOTION TO STRIKE 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT RE THE PUNITIVE DAMAGES ALLEGATIONS IN THE SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT FILED BY PLAINTIFF THOMAS MILLER IS DENIED IN ITS ENTIRETY. MR. MILLER HAS SUFFICIENTLY ALLEGED THAT EFOLDER, THROUGH ITS CHIEF OPERATING OFFICER, HAD ADVANCE KNOWLEDGE OF MR. RICHMAN'S UNFITNESS AS A SALESPERSON FOR EFOLDER AND THAT HE POSED AN UNREASONABLE RISK OF HARM TO OTHERS WITH WHOM HE WOULD COME INTO CONTACT WITH DURING THE COURSE OF HIS EMPLOYMENT AT EFOLDER. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: CAROL KAREN CSR #8189 =302/HK

DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-08-2017 Overruled
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
DEFENDANT EFOLDER, INC's DEMURRER to 2ND Amended COMPLAINT - Defendant eFolder, Inc.'s demurrer to all three causes of action in the second amended complaint filed by plaintiff Thomas Miller is overruled as to all three causes of action. Mr. Miller alleges sufficient facts to support liability of eFolder based on respondeat superior for the intentional torts allegedly committed by Mr. Richman. eFolder acknowledges that Mr. Miller has adequately alleged facts supporting an instrumentality theory, but argues that the personal malice exception applies to preclude respondeat superior liability. The facts alleged in the second amended complaint do not necessarily show that Mr. Richman's misconduct resulted from any personal malice he harbored against Mr. Miller. eFolder's argument that "The only logical conclusion here is that the act of striking plaintiff was one of personal malice" (Reply at 4:2-3) is incorrect. The facts alleged in the second amended complaint also reasonably support the conclusion that Mr. Richman struck Mr. Miller because he was intoxicated and personal malice (or at least unrelated to Mr. Richman's employment at eFolder) played no role in the assault and thus, at the pleading stage, this case looks similar to Rodgers v. Kemper Construction Co. (1975) 50 Cal. App. 3d 608 (cited with approval in Lisa M.) which affirmed a verdict based on respondeat superior for an alcohol-fueled fight by an off-duty employee against a non-employee where the assailant and victim were "complete strangers" until they were brought together by the assailant's work. Mr. Miller has also sufficiently alleged a claim for negligent hiring, retention, and supervision of Mr. Richman per Federico v. Superior Court (1997) 59 Cal.App.4th 1207, 1214. The second amended complaint contains ample facts showing that eFolder knew facts about Mr. Richman's background which would warn a reasonable person that Mr. Richman presented an undue risk of harm to others who would come into contact with him in settings where eFolder facilitated the excessive consumption of alcohol. Order signed. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Carol Karen CSR #8189 =302/hk

MOTION TO STRIKE 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-08-2017 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
DEFENDANT EFOLDER, INC.'s MOTION TO STRIKE 2ND Amended COMPLAINT - Defendant eFolder, Inc.'s motion to strike the punitive damages allegations in the second amended complaint filed by plaintiff Thomas Miller is denied in its entirety. Mr. Miller has sufficiently alleged that eFolder, through its chief operating officer, had advance knowledge of Mr. Richman's unfitness as a salesperson for eFolder and that he posed an unreasonable risk of harm to others with whom he would come into contact with during the course of his employment at eFolder. Order signed. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Carol Karen CSR #8189 =302/hk

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-30-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
William A. Logan, Jr., of Logan Mooney LLP, Tel. 415.738.0764, for plaintiffs Thomas Miller;
Steven Myers, of Law Offices of John A. Biard, Tel. 925.945.4218, for defendants Efolder, Inc., appearing via CourtCall.

Ruling: A R G U E D. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT EFOLDER, INC. DEMURRER TO ENTIRE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is sustained with twenty days leave to amend. Mr. Miller is given leave to amend his complaint to allege actions that occurred within the scope of Mr. Richman's employment and that Mr. Richman was unfit to work as a salesperson, if he can do so in good faith. Order signed. Judge: A. James Robertson II; Clerk: Felicia Green; Court Reporter: Gena Eales, CSR No. 9176, Email: geales@sftc.org; Dept. 302.
